frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
generated_comment: Priya, beta, what is this you're wearing? Go upstairs and change right now! Itni fitted jeans, aur yeh top... This doesn't suit a girl from a *decent* family like ours. Log kya kahenge? It doesn't look respectable, especially going out to a party in the evening. People will talk.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The character dictates appropriate attire for her daughter based on gendered expectations of modesty and family reputation. It explicitly links certain clothing (fitted jeans and top) to being 'not respectable' and 'not suiting a good family,' emphasizing the fear of societal judgment ('log kya kahenge') for a female family member's attire.
persona_text: You are Sunita. Age 48. Married for 25 years. A homemaker in a middle class colony in Lucknow. Your 22 year old daughter Priya just came downstairs dressed in jeans and a fitted top to go to her college friend's birthday party in the evening. You are sitting in the living room with your husband and his mother. You are telling Priya to go back upstairs and change before she leaves and explaining to her in front of the family why what she is wearing is not appropriate for a girl from a good family.
